name: [Player Name]
description: The Watcher's name.

name: [Player Race]
description: The Watcher's race (e.g. "elf").
notes: Deadfire also replaces the lowercase form [player race] with the lower-cased value for mid-sentence use; PoE1 has no lowercase pairs — matching is exact-case.

name: [Player Subrace]
description: The Watcher's subrace (e.g. "wood elf").
notes: Deadfire also replaces the lowercase form [player subrace]; PoE1 does not.

name: [Player Class]
description: The Watcher's class (gendered form in PoE1).
example: "This one's the softest [player class] in Deadfire."
notes: Deadfire also replaces the lowercase form [player class]; PoE1 does not. The single shipped use is the lowercase variant.

name: [Player Culture]
description: The Watcher's culture (e.g. "Aedyr").
example: "Then you will also share my name when you take this back to [Player Culture]?"
notes: Deadfire also replaces the lowercase form [player culture]; PoE1 does not.

name: [Player Background]
description: The Watcher's background (e.g. "aristocrat").
notes: Deadfire also replaces the lowercase form [player background].

name: [Player Deity]
description: The priest player's chosen deity.

name: [Player Paladin Order]
description: The paladin player's order (gendered form).

name: [Player Ship]
description: The player's ship name.

name: [Player Animal Companion]
description: The ranger player's animal companion's name.
example: "[Player Animal Companion] and I don't mind trudging through the hatchery."

name: [Specified n]
description: The character bound to the conversation's Specified Speaker slot n (script-selected, e.g. a specific companion).
example: [Specified 0] takes a firm grip on the rope and descends.
notes: Shipped text uses n = 0–5 (Deadfire) and 0–1 (PoE1); any bound slot works.

name: [SkillCheck n]
description: The party member selected by skill-check slot n (highest relevant skill).
example: Partway through, [SkillCheck 0] starts to lag behind.

name: [Slot n]
description: The character in party slot n (0–5). PoE1 party banter uses this heavily; the Deadfire engine supports it equally.

name: [ShipDuel_Player]
description: The player captain's name during a ship duel.

name: [ShipDuel_PlayerShip]
description: The player ship's name during a ship duel.

name: [ShipDuel_Opponent]
description: The enemy captain's name during a ship duel.

name: [ShipDuel_OpponentShip]
description: The enemy ship's name during a ship duel.
example: The crew of [ShipDuel_OpponentShip] spring into action.

name: [ShipDuel_SurrenderCost]
description: The cost the opponent demands for surrender.

name: [ShipDuel_CloseToBoardCost]
description: The cost/distance to close for boarding.

name: [ShipDuel_PlayerFullSailDist]
description: The distance the player ship moves at full sail.

name: [ShipDuel_PlayerHalfSailDist]
description: The distance the player ship moves at half sail.

name: [ShipDuel_FleeChance]
description: The chance (percentage) that fleeing succeeds.

name: [ShipDuel_BraceChance]
description: The chance (percentage) that bracing prevents injuries.

name: [Interaction Ability]
description: Display name of the ability granted by a scripted interaction.

name: [NPCBacker]
description: Backer NPC description text.

name: [God_Boon]
description: Name of the god's boon being granted.

name: …
description: Italics — narration emphasis, foreign words, thoughts. Pairs must be closed.

name: <ispeech>…</ispeech>
description: "Inner speech" styling — telepathy, soul-voices, gods.

name: <xg>…</xg>
description: Dialect styling for single words.

name: <color="…">…</color>
description: Text colour.

name: <link="glossary://…">…</link>
description: Hover link to a glossary entry (ship actions, deities).

name: <link="stringtooltip://…">…</link>
description: Hover tooltip from a tooltip stringtable (untranslated speeches, cyclopedia notes).

name: <link="neutralvalue://…">…</link>
description: Hover translation of an in-world-language line; the text before the colon names the language.
example: [Vailian] <link="neutralvalue://Vailian: Do you speak Vailian, sir?">"Perla Vailian, fentre?"</link>
notes: A few shipped lines are missing the closing quote on the attribute — parse leniently.

name: <sprite="Inline" name="…" tint=1>
description: Inline icon (ship-combat action buttons); self-closing.

name: Stage directions
description: A bracketed action in a player response is a non-spoken option, rendered literally: [Say nothing.], [Attack], [Leave]. It may stand alone or prefix speech. Free text, not a fixed vocabulary (~1,300 distinct values in shipped data).
example: [Lie] "He kept the koīki for himself and paid for my silence."

name: Language markers
description: A leading bracket names the in-world language of the quoted line, usually paired with a neutralvalue:// hover translation. Seen for Vailian, Huana, Rauataian, Engwithan, Ixamitl, Eld Aedyran, Ordhjóma, Lembur.

name: VO / chatter annotations
description: PoE1 companion chatter describes non-verbal audio in brackets — subtitle placeholders for barks, not dialog options.

name: Skill & disposition labels
description: Option labels like [Diplomacy] or [Honest] shown in-game are built by the engine from the node's check data — they are never written in dialog text. Don't type them.
